message:
feat(extension-host): track quarantine mesh unreachable peers and report degraded propagation

Extends the DelegateCell quarantine mesh system to distinguish between
attempted, successful, and failed mesh propagation targets. Previously,
quarantine propagation was all-or-nothing — if any peer was configured,
propagation was reported as successful. Now the system tracks unreachable
peers explicitly and reports accurate degraded/failed outcomes.

Key changes:
- Add quarantine_mesh_unreachable_peers BTreeSet to DelegateCell for
  tracking peers that cannot be reached during mesh propagation
- Add set_quarantine_mesh_unreachable_peers() and accessor methods
- Split mesh propagation into mesh_attempted_targets, mesh_targets
  (successful), and mesh_failed_targets in ContainmentWorkflowLogEntry
  and DelegateCellEvidence::ContainmentAction
- Add DELEGATE_MESH_PROPAGATION_DEGRADED_ERROR_CODE ("FE-DELEGATE-0008")
- Emit "delegate_quarantine_mesh_partial_propagation" when some peers
  fail, or "delegate_quarantine_mesh_propagation_failed" when all fail
- Report outcome as "degraded" with error code when failures occur
- Add comprehensive tests: partial failure and total failure scenarios
- Existing tests updated with new field assertions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
